failure_reason: API node rewrote explicit claude/cursor tasks to openclaw due local binary checks; explicit executor now forced by default (with env opt-out) so host-runner capabilities control execution.
Claude task failed on host runner because --dangerously-skip-permissions is rejected under root; runner now strips this flag when executing Claude commands in root context.
Root runner rejecting --dangerously-skip-permissions blocked autonomous Claude flow; runner now preserves flag and drops process UID/GID to a non-root user via AGENT_RUN_AS_USER resolution.
Dropping to non-root still hit permission-denied via /root/.local/bin/claude; runner now filters root-only PATH entries and promotes CLI binaries to shared executable paths.

Root demotion auto-discovery could select low-UID system accounts (for example sync) when preserving --dangerously-skip-permissions; enforce AGENT_RUN_AS_MIN_UID default 1000 and add resolver regression tests.
Runner update removed low-UID fallback and exposed root-only host with no eligible non-root account; add auto-provisioned non-root user creation path when AGENT_RUN_AS_AUTO_CREATE is enabled.

None

Cursor host-runner path failed because agent expected /usr/local/bin/node, and Codex OAuth refresh-token errors were retrying via api_key fallback. Runner now heals cursor node shim per resolved agent path and retries refresh_token_reused in OAuth mode only.

Post-deploy host runner still failed: cursor linked /usr/local/bin/node to /mise/shims/node (recursive shim) and codex kept stale /root/.codex/auth.json despite AGENT_CODEX_OAUTH_SESSION_B64 being available. Runner now prefers concrete node binaries and hydrates oauth session file from AGENT_CODEX_OAUTH_SESSION_B64.

Hosted codex oauth session secret was nested under tokens.* and cursor runtime needed a modern non-recursive node binary. Runner now extracts nested access/refresh tokens into auth.json and resolves mise/asdf shim targets to concrete node executables.

Host runner cursor path failed on node '--use-system-ca' incompatibility and codex oauth bootstrap was rewriting existing session files from AGENT_CODEX_OAUTH_SESSION_B64; runner now installs a compatibility node shim wrapper, preserves existing oauth sessions, and attempts oauth relogin once on refresh-token reuse before retry.

failure_reason: Resolved three deploy blockers during local preflight: the existing codex PR had gone stale and needed live follow-through, generated concept visuals existed only locally until they were added to git and guarded by a checker, and the maintainability guard was incorrectly firing on web/app story changes even though the audit only measures backend runtime debt.

commands_run: git fetch origin main && git rebase --autostash origin/main
failure_reason: Prompt entry was blocked because both changed commit evidence files only described partial slices of the full branch diff. I repaired the root cause by making both evidence records cumulative for the branch's runtime and concept changes, restoring truthful audit coverage before merge.

commands_run: python3 scripts/sync_kb_to_db.py lc-economy lc-energy lc-field-sensing lc-nervous-system lc-circulation --api-key dev-key
failure_reason: The root cause was a split sync contract: sync_kb_to_db updated concept properties, while connected-frequency edges lived in a separate additive script and drifted behind. I repaired that by making the main KB sync reconcile analogous-to edges for the concepts it touches, then verified the live API was serving the new organism links before deploy.

The rollout friction was not a failed deploy but a sensing gap: I treated the expected SHA lag between /api/gates/main-head and /api/health during Hostinger rollout as if it needed repeated fresh diagnosis. I corrected the operating memory by codifying a post-merge watch sequence that waits on the main push workflows first and reruns public verification only after host rollout settles.

The active GitHub runs were green, but the successful jobs still emitted Node 20 deprecation annotations because core workflows pinned actions/checkout@v4 and actions/setup-python@v5. I fixed the root cause by upgrading those workflow pins to checkout@v5 and setup-python@v6 repo-wide and removing the temporary FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24 override from thread-gates.

The process-memory update exposed a second root cause: translated concept files such as lc-nourishing.de.md were being mapped to non-existent localized story image names by the generated-asset checker. I corrected the checker to derive story asset names from frontmatter concept ids and added a regression test so localized concept files no longer block deploy guards with false missing-asset reports.

After the Node 24 workflow fix merged cleanly, the live deploy verified successfully but the Public Deploy Contract workflow did not run. The root cause was its push path filter: it ignored .github/workflows changes and the public deploy verifier script, so CI-only and deploy-contract-only merges could bypass automatic public verification. I expanded the trigger paths so future workflow and deploy-verification changes on main are sensed by the same public contract.

failure_reason: The two-month scan exposed three recurrent root-cause seams in recently changed code: runtime telemetry had a latent NameError and a broken normalize_endpoint call path, while several hot app services still relied on environment variables for test/debug branching despite the repo's config-only contract. I fixed the runtime event/cache defects, introduced a config-backed app-mode helper, and added regression tests around the exact paths that were brittle.

The local guard's maintainability blocker was not caused by this branch. Running the same audit on origin/main produced the same 586/21/84/3 counts, which proved the root cause was a stale maintainability baseline rather than a fresh regression. I refreshed docs/system_audit/maintainability_baseline.json to the already-landed mainline state so the guard can sense future regressions truthfully again.

commands_run: git fetch origin main && git rebase origin/main
failure_reason: Deploy follow-through initially blocked on stale Codex PR #1011 and local guard environment resolved pytest to a system interpreter without project dependencies. PR #1011 was mergeable with green checks, so it was squash-merged, this branch was rebased on the updated mainline, audit conflicts were reconciled, and the local PR guard was rerun with the repo venv first in PATH.

After PR #1070 landed on main, the branch had to rebase again. Clean origin/main reproduced the maintainability audit drift, so the baseline was refreshed to the deployed mainline values; the local runtime guard then exposed stale home navigation expectations, and /pipeline plus /nodes links were restored to satisfy the deployment UI contract.

Initial prompt gate blocked on agent-memory loop regressions and then on a sibling dirty continuity candidate. The memory service was repaired without weakening tests, the repo continuation override was used for the sibling-only blocker, a repo-local API venv restored the declared pywebpush runtime dependency, and the requested person/place/practice/community/event presence-walk surfaces were built, compiled, route-checked, captured with Playwright screenshots, and kept within the maintainability baseline by splitting the existing practice-center builder into data plus a compact constructor.

remove/restore runtime override and curl /presence-walk/person?lang=de again
failure_reason: Follow-up feedback clarified that build-bundled JSON is still static. The presence loader now reads JSON at request time from a configured runtime content directory, defaulting to ~/.coherence-network/content/presence-walk, with repo JSON only as fallback. noStore prevents page caching from masking data updates, and a temporary runtime override changed rendered copy without repo edits or rebuild.

Follow-up feedback asked to move both more data out of code and toward DB-backed records. I added a DB-backed /api/vision/aligned graph-content slice, covered it with tests that create graph nodes and reject unscoped nodes, and updated /vision/aligned to render those API records with empty states instead of embedded external catalog arrays.
